title: lomatium triternatum var. alatum
intro: Lomatium triternatum var. alatum, also known as meadow bane or fern-leaved lomatium, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. Its roots and rhizomes contain sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Lomatium triternatum var. alatum have been shown to have antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal effects, making it beneficial for treating respiratory infections, colds, and flu, as well as other conditions such as bronchitis and sinusitis. Herbal preparations of Lomatium triternatum var. alatum are commonly used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
